behavior_summary: U8P_FLORSPIK::gotHit handles the item being struck: it guards against duplicate processing using a per-item Q flag, switches the item's type to a 'hit' variant, spawns a visual effect, runs a short frame animation sequence, then either applies damage to an NPC target (if the incoming parameter refers to a living NPC) or hurls the item with randomized velocity. After handling it sets a final item type and clears the per-item Q flag.

The routine uses Item::getQ/Item::setQ as a reentrancy/processing guard, Item::setType and Item::setFrame to change visuals, spawn->FREE::func2A12 to create effects, spawn->FREE::func1C19 as a short animation/tick delay, and either METHOD::receiveHitWrapper to deliver damage to an NPC or Item::hurl to throw the item.
notable_control_flow: Early exit if Item::getQ(this) != 0 to avoid duplicate handling.
On first entry, Item::setQ(this, 0x01) sets a per-item guard; cleared at end with Item::setQ(this, 0x00).
Item::setType(this, 0x0339) is called immediately after setting the Q flag to switch to a 'hit' visual/state; later Item::setType(this, 0x033A) sets a subsequent/final type.
A short animation loop increments local2 from 0 up to 4 calling Item::setFrame(this, frameIndex) with small suspends via spawn->FREE::func1C19 between frames.

Branch: if the incoming parameter (stored to local1) refers to an NPC and Npc::isDead(addressOf(local3)) reports 'not dead', call METHOD::receiveHitWrapper to apply randomized damage; otherwise call Item::hurl with randomized velocities and a target/location pointer derived from local1.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of the numeric type constants (0x0339, 0x033A) and effect ids (0x10, 0x09) — likely visual/behavior variants but exact mapping is uncertain.
Precise role of the first parameter (uword, declared as param1) — inferred as the hitting actor or hit target reference but not certain.
Meaning of some numeric flags passed into Item::hurl (0x04, 0x0F) — inferred use/semantics from common engine patterns but not explicitly labeled.
Whether the animation loop always runs (both branches) or differs subtly by branch — inferred to run in both but raw nodes leave small uncertainty.

main_function.name: U8P_FLORSPIK::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Process the item being hit: prevent re-entrant handling, change visuals, spawn effects, animate a short frame sequence, then either apply damage to a valid NPC target or hurl the item; finally restore final type and clear the per-item processing flag.
entry_conditions: This process is invoked when the item receives a hit (engine calls process U8P_FLORSPIK::gotHit).
Function proceeds only if Item::getQ(this) == 0 (per-item Q flag clear).

external_calls.purpose: Read per-item 'Q' flag to detect and avoid double-processing/reentrancy.
Set (0x01) the per-item processing guard on entry and clear (0x00) it on exit.
Switch the item's type/state to visual/behavior variants (0x0339 on hit, 0x033A after handling).
Spawn a visual/particle effect associated with the hit (observed effect ids 0x10 and 0x09).
Short delay/animation-tick used between Item::setFrame updates; execution suspends after this call.
Advance item animation frames during a brief sequence driven by a small counter (local2).
Check whether the candidate target NPC is dead; used to decide whether to apply damage or hurl the item.
Deliver randomized small damage/hit to a valid alive NPC target (called with urandom-derived damage and hit-type).
Launch/hurl the item with flags and randomized x/y velocity when no valid alive NPC target is present.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_FLORSPIK::gotHit handles the item being struck: it guards against duplicate processing using a per-item Q flag, switches the item's type to a 'hit' variant, spawns a visual effect, runs a short frame animation sequence, then either applies damage to an NPC target (if the incoming parameter refers to a living NPC) or hurls the item with randomized velocity. After handling it sets a final item type and clears the per-item Q flag.
